title: Intensely Roasted Portuguese Pork Bao
description: A slow-cooked, dairy-free Portuguese dish featuring roasted pork and springy buns, served in a bento box. This show-off dish is perfect for special occasions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, olive oil, garlic, and ginger.
Place the pork shoulder in a large Dutch oven and brush the soy sauce mixture all over the pork.
Cover the Dutch oven with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 6 hours, or until it is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
While the pork is roasting, prepare the baos according to the package instructions or your homemade recipe.
Once the pork is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before shredding it with two forks.
Assemble the bao by splitting it in half and filling it with shredded pork, scallions, and sesame seeds.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the intensely roasted Portuguese pork bao in a bento box.
